verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 is a type II transmembrane protein with a stable plasma membrane pool where it functions as an oligosaccharide-binding receptor for cell-cell and cell-matrix interactions. It has its own TM anchor (short N-terminal hydrophobic domain that remains uncleaved) and stably localizes to the outer leaflet via its intrinsic mechanism, despite its dominant Golgi resident role. The surface pool is documented by immunofluorescence and functional assays across multiple cell types. The protein reaches the outer leaflet by its own TM-mediated mechanism.
